title: Frontend Foundations
description: Frontend foundations course for Masterschool's internal engineers Bootcamp. Covers TypeScript and React from first principles, ending with a Frontend Chat MVP built in plain React + Vite that the Backend course will plug into in Week 3.

lessons.title: Why TypeScript? Types as Documentation and Safety
outcomes: Articulate why TypeScript is preferred over plain JavaScript at scale
Explain how static types catch bugs before runtime and act as documentation

lessons.title: TypeScript Fundamentals: Types, Interfaces, Generics
outcomes: Define primitive types, union types, and object types
Write interfaces and type aliases for real domain models
Use generics to write a simple generic function or type

lessons.title: From JavaScript to TypeScript in Practice
outcomes: Convert a small JavaScript module to TypeScript with proper typing
Avoid any and write explicit return types
Read and respond to TypeScript compiler errors confidently

lessons.title: What React Is (And Why It Exists): Components, Declarative UI, Virtual DOM
outcomes: Explain what problem React solves compared to direct DOM manipulation
Explain components as units of UI and the declarative rendering model

lessons.title: Setting Up a React + TypeScript Project with Vite
outcomes: Scaffold and run a React + TypeScript project using Vite
Explain the basic project structure and tsconfig settings

lessons.title: JSX: Markup as Code
outcomes: Read and write JSX with embedded expressions, conditional rendering, and lists
Explain that JSX is function calls and how it compiles

lessons.title: Components and Props (Typed)
outcomes: Define functional components with typed props
Compose components and pass typed data between them
Use and type children properly

lessons.title: Component State with useState
outcomes: Manage local component state with useState
Explain the difference between state and props
Avoid common pitfalls such as stale state and mutating state directly

lessons.title: Handling Events and Forms (Controlled Inputs)
outcomes: Wire event handlers with proper TypeScript typing
Build a controlled form with multiple inputs and validation

lessons.title: Side Effects with useEffect (And When to Avoid Them)
outcomes: Use useEffect for genuine side effects such as subscriptions, fetches, and timers
Explain the dependency array and cleanup functions
Identify common anti-patterns and when not to reach for useEffect

lessons.title: Lifting State Up and Sharing State Between Components
outcomes: Identify when state should be lifted to a common ancestor
Refactor a multi-component feature so state lives in the right place

lessons.title: Composition Patterns: Children, Slots, Compound Components
outcomes: Build flexible reusable components using children and render-prop style composition
Prefer composition over configuration props for reusability

lessons.title: Custom Hooks: Extracting and Reusing Logic
outcomes: Extract stateful logic into a custom hook with a clean API
Explain the rules of hooks and why they exist

lessons.title: Context API: Sharing State Without Prop Drilling
outcomes: Create and consume a Context with proper TypeScript typing
Explain when Context is the right tool versus when it is overkill

lessons.title: Final Project - React + TypeScript Todos App
outcomes: Build a typed React app from scratch using Vite
Demonstrate clean component decomposition, lifted state, and at least one custom hook
Deliver the project as a GitHub PR with passing TypeScript compilation

lessons.title: Clean Code Principles for React Components
outcomes: Identify smells in React components such as long files, mixed concerns, and deep nesting
Refactor a messy component into smaller, single-responsibility units

lessons.title: Component Architecture: Container vs Presentational, Pure Components
outcomes: Separate stateful container components from pure presentational components
Explain why pure components are easier to test and reuse

lessons.title: useReducer: When State Logic Outgrows useState
outcomes: Model state as actions and a reducer for non-trivial flows
Explain when useReducer is preferable to multiple useState hooks

lessons.title: Performance: When and Why to Use useMemo and useCallback
outcomes: Identify the cost of unnecessary re-renders
Apply useMemo and useCallback only where they actually help
Measure render behavior with the React DevTools Profiler

lessons.title: React.memo and Re-render Behavior
outcomes: Explain React reconciliation and what triggers re-renders
Apply React.memo to expensive presentational components when justified

lessons.title: Fetching Data from APIs in React
outcomes: Call a REST API from React and store the result in state
Cancel stale requests and avoid race conditions

lessons.title: Handling Loading, Empty, and Error States Cleanly
outcomes: Handle loading, empty, success, and error states explicitly in async UIs
Write predictable UIs for users even when the network fails

lessons.title: Designing a Chat UI: Components and Data Flow
outcomes: Decompose a chat UI into a clean component tree
Design typed data shapes for users, conversations, and messages on the frontend

lessons.title: Building the Message List and Message Composer
outcomes: Implement an auto-scrolling message list with proper key handling
Implement a message composer with controlled input and submit-on-Enter

lessons.title: Optimistic Updates and Local State Sync
outcomes: Apply optimistic updates for fast-feeling UIs
Handle rollback when the server rejects an optimistic action

lessons.title: Mocking a Backend API for Frontend Development
outcomes: Build a mocked REST API on the frontend that matches the real backend contract
Design the contract first so frontend and backend can develop in parallel

lessons.title: Frontend Testing Fundamentals (Vitest + React Testing Library)
outcomes: Explain the testing pyramid and what is worth testing on the frontend
Set up Vitest + React Testing Library in a Vite project

lessons.title: Writing Tests for Components and Custom Hooks
outcomes: Write tests that interact with components from the user's perspective
Test a custom hook in isolation

lessons.title: Reviewing and Refactoring: Clean Code Pass on the Chat MVP
outcomes: Review code critically against Clean Code principles
Apply targeted refactors that improve readability and maintainability without changing behavior

lessons.title: Final Project - Frontend Chat MVP (React + Vite + Mocked API)
outcomes: Ship a Frontend Chat MVP using React + Vite + TypeScript against a mocked API contract
Demonstrate clean component architecture, explicit loading/error/empty states, and at least one custom hook
Deliver the project as a GitHub PR with passing tests and TypeScript compilation
